metadata.title:
HOW ALLEN HELPED


TO Reflect DOAK

pages.raw_text:
Notwithstanding the reelection
Of Sheriff Doak by good majority,
F. M. Allen, business agent of the
local electrical workers union,
wants to make it plain that he at
no time made any settlement of the
grievances of his union against the
sheriff for appointing men as dep
uties who were employed by the
Washington Water Power company
to fill the place of the striking
linemen. The reason Mr.. Allen
makes this statement is due to the
fact that Doak circulated report
during the last few days OF the
campaign that all differences be-
tween him and the union had been
settled.


1t might be as we'll to explain
that Mr.. Allen and many other
members of his union were not in
position to do Mr.. Doak any
great damage, as they had got into
Mr.. Corbin's habit of not register
ing. and were unable to vote.
